Title: Add New Catalog
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Catalog module accessible
Steps:
1. Open Catalog module > Add > Click Catalog
2. Verify screen displays with required fields
3. Fill Catalog name field (required)
4. Configure Language block if needed
5. Click Create
Expected Result:
- System displays new catalog screen
- Catalog name field marked as required
- Language block visible and functional
- System saves new catalog successfully
- New catalog appears in catalog list

Title: Edit Existing Catalog
Preconditions: Admin logged in, at least one catalog exists
Steps:
1. Right-click on existing catalog > Manage
2. Verify edit screen displays
3. Review blocks: Name (required), Language, Property
4. Modify catalog name
5. Change language settings
6. Modify property settings
7. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Edit screen displays with catalog data
- Name field is required
- Language block shows current selection
- Property block displays correctly
- System saves all changes
- Updated catalog reflects changes

Title: Delete Catalog - Cancel
Preconditions: Admin logged in, multiple catalogs exist
Steps:
1. Right-click on any catalog > Delete
2. Verify confirmation dialog appears
3. Click Cancel
Expected Result:
- Confirmation dialog displays
- System does not delete catalog
- Catalog remains in list
- No data loss

Title: Delete Catalog - Confirm
Preconditions: Admin logged in, test catalog exists
Steps:
1. Right-click on test catalog > Delete
2. Verify confirmation dialog
3. Type 'Yes' in confirmation field
4. Click Confirm
Expected Result:
- Confirmation dialog requires typing 'Yes'
- System deletes catalog after confirmation
- Catalog removed from list
- Related data handled appropriately

Title: Create New Configurable Product in Admin Catalog
Preconditions: Admin SPA accessible at ${BACK_URL}. Logged in as ${ADMIN} with catalog edit permissions. Products with Options > Configurations category exists.
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} and log in as admin
2. Navigate to Catalog module
3. Navigate to the Products with Options category or equivalent test category
4. Click Add item or Add product
5. Select product type Configurable (or type that supports configurations)
6. Enter product name: QA Test Configurable Product
7. Enter product SKU: QA-CONFIG-TEST-001
8. Set product price to $100.00
9. Save the product (click Save or OK)
10. Verify product appears in category list
Expected Result:
- Admin SPA loads and dashboard is visible
- Catalog module opens
- Configurable product form displayed
- Name and SKU fields populated
- Product saved successfully with no error messages
- QA Test Configurable Product visible in category list

Title: Add Option Products to Configuration Section

7. Save the option

Expected Result:

- Product search/selector opens
- Product found in search results
- Product added as option
- Quantity and price set correctly
- First option saved without error
- Second option added successfully
- Both options visible in the configuration section

- Quantity field updated to 3
- Price field updated to $25.00

- Updated values displayed
- Extended price calculation is correct

Title: Mark Configuration Section as Required and Verify Storefront Enforcement
Preconditions: QA Test Configurable Product with configuration section exists. Admin logged in. Product published and accessible on storefront.
Steps:
1. Open the QA Test Options configuration section in Admin
2. Change the required flag to true/on
3. Save the section
4. Navigate to the configurable product on storefront
5. Observe the accordion label
6. Set quantity to 1 without selecting any option (with None selected)
7. Attempt to click Add to cart
8. Select an option from the section
9. Click Add to cart
Expected Result:
- Section edit opens
- Required flag enabled
- Saved without error
- Product PDP loads
- Accordion subtitle no longer says (optional) or shows (required)
- Quantity set to 1
- Validation error: Please select a required option or similar; item NOT added to cart
- Option selected
- Item successfully added to cart

Title: Add Tax Type to Product

3. Scroll to or navigate to the Properties tab
4. Locate the Tax Type field
5. Click the Tax Type dropdown
6. Select an available tax type from the list
7. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Tax Type dropdown is visible on the product form
- Available tax types are listed in the dropdown
- Selected tax type is saved successfully
- Product record shows correct tax type after save
- No validation errors appear

Title: Category Image Upload

Title: Product Automatic Links

- Each link shows the correct catalog and category reference
- Clicking a link navigates to the correct location
- No broken or duplicate links appear

Title: Category Automatic Links

Expected Result:

Title: Links from Category and Product

- Navigation is consistent between category and product links

Title: Full-Text Search Within Catalog Module
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), catalog populated with at least 20 products and categories

5. Clear the search field and verify all items return
6. Enter a category name and verify filtering applies to categories
7. Enter a non-existent term and verify an appropriate empty-state message
Expected Result:
- Search bar is present and accepts text input
- Entering a term filters the product/category list to matching items
- Clearing the search restores the full list
- Category name search filters categories correctly
- Non-existent term shows an empty state or No results message

Title: Clone BOM Product
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), a BOM (Bill of Materials) product exists with at least one component
Steps:

2. Locate and open a BOM product
3. Note the product name, properties, and BOM components
4. Click the Clone or Copy action (from toolbar or context menu)
5. Confirm the clone action in the dialog
6. Wait for the clone to complete
7. Open the cloned product from the list
Expected Result:
- Clone action is available on BOM products
- Cloned product is created with a distinct name (e.g. Copy of original name)
- All top-level product properties are copied to the clone
- BOM component list is present in the cloned product
- Clone has a new unique product ID
- No errors occur during the clone operation

Title: Clone BOM Product Variations
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), a BOM product with variations (child products) exists

3. Initiate the Clone action
4. Confirm the clone action
5. Open the cloned product
6. Navigate to the Variations tab of the cloned product
7. Verify the cloned variations match the originals in count and properties
Expected Result:
- Cloned BOM product includes all variations from the original
- Each variation is a new record with a unique ID
- Variation properties (name, SKU, images) are copied correctly
- Cloned variations are editable independently of the original
- No variation data is lost or corrupted during cloning

Title: Virtual Catalog Assignment

7. Verify the product appears in the virtual catalog category

- Product search returns physical catalog products

- Assigned product is visible in the virtual catalog without duplicating the original

Title: Dynamic Product Associations
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), Associations feature enabled, multiple products exist
Steps:
1. Navigate to Catalog module and open a product
2. Navigate to the Associations tab
3. Click Add Dynamic Association or configure an association group
4. Define association criteria (e.g. same category, same brand)
5. Save the association rule
6. Verify the associated products list populates based on the rule
7. Verify associated products update if catalog changes match the rule
Expected Result:

- Dynamic association rule can be created with criteria

- Association list refreshes when matching criteria are met

Title: Product Required Properties Validation
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), catalog schema has at least one property marked as Required
Steps:
1. Navigate to Catalog module and open or create a product
2. Navigate to the Properties tab
3. Identify a property marked as Required (indicated by asterisk or label)
4. Clear or leave the required property field empty
5. Attempt to Save the product
6. Observe validation feedback
7. Fill in the required property and save again
Expected Result:
- Required properties are visually marked (asterisk or highlight)
- Saving with empty required property shows a validation error message
- Error message identifies which property is missing
- Product does not save while required fields are empty
- After filling required field, product saves successfully
- No data loss occurs for non-required fields during failed save attempt

Title: Text Option Properties
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), Catalog module with property management access
Steps:
1. Navigate to Catalog module > Properties or open a catalog property blade
2. Click Add Property
3. Select ShortText or Text as the property value type
4. Set property display name and configure it as an Option (not free-text)
5. Add at least 3 predefined option values
6. Save the property
7. Assign it to a product and verify the option values appear as a dropdown
Expected Result:
- Property is created with text type and predefined options
- Option values are saved and listed correctly
- On a product, the property appears as a dropdown with the defined options
- Only predefined options are selectable (no free-text entry)
- Selecting an option and saving the product persists the value

Title: Property Name Rules Validation
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (${BACK_URL}), Catalog module with property creation access
Steps:
1. Navigate to Catalog module > Properties blade
2. Click Add Property
3. Attempt to enter invalid characters in the property Name field (e.g. spaces, special chars like @#!%)
4. Observe whether validation prevents saving
5. Attempt to save the property with an invalid name
6. Observe the error message
7. Enter a valid property name (alphanumeric, underscores, hyphens only)
8. Save and verify success
Expected Result:
- Invalid characters in property name trigger a validation error
- Error message clearly states the name rules (e.g. Only alphanumeric, underscore, and hyphen allowed)
- Property cannot be saved with an invalid name
- Valid name allows the property to be created successfully
- Existing properties with valid names are not affected
